title: simsia ovata
intro: Simsia ovata, also known as the wild marigold, is a plant species within the asteraceae family, native to tropical regions of Central and South America. Traditionally, Simsia ovata has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat wounds, skin irritations, and infections. The plant's health benefits include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and serve as a natural remedy for respiratory problems. Simsia ovata is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and topical creams, which are prepared by drying the leaves and flowers of the plant to preserve its medicinal properties.
